checks.describe: the deep-dive relies on llama-server answering (the batching-scheduler and prefix-cache reads run against it). Self-seeds the stack if down
checks.run: curl -fsS --max-time 3 http://127.0.0.1:8080/health >/dev/null 2>&1 || make -C labs/opsmate up >/dev/null 2>&1; curl -s --max-time 5 http://127.0.0.1:8080/health | grep -c 'ok' | xargs -I{} sh -c 'if [ {} -ge 1 ]; then echo engine-up; else echo engine-down; fi'

checks.describe: the requests_processing and requests_deferred gauges the scheduler-loop section reads are exposed and parseable (section 2)
checks.run: curl -fsS --max-time 3 http://127.0.0.1:8080/health >/dev/null 2>&1 || make -C labs/opsmate up >/dev/null 2>&1; curl -s --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:8080/metrics | grep -Ec 'llamacpp:requests_(processing|deferred) ' | xargs -I{} sh -c 'if [ {} -ge 2 ]; then echo gauges-ok; else echo gauges-missing; fi'

checks.describe: a repeated identical system prefix is served from the prefix cache — cached_tokens is 0 on the first call and non-zero on the second (section 4). Asserts the repeat is cached, never a fixed count
checks.run: curl -fsS --max-time 3 http://127.0.0.1:8080/health >/dev/null 2>&1 || make -C labs/opsmate up >/dev/null 2>&1; BODY='{"model":"qwen3-0.6b","messages":[{"role":"system","content":"You are OpsMate, an SRE assistant. Follow the runbook. Be concise. /no_think"},{"role":"user","content":"Say ready."}]}'; curl -s --max-time 60 http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/chat/completions -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "$BODY" >/dev/null 2>&1; C=$(curl -s --max-time 60 http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/chat/completions -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d "$BODY" | jq -r '.usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens // 0'); if [ "$C" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then echo prefix-cached; else echo prefix-uncached; fi
